deployment_rules.production_server: Use ONLY on production deployment machines. Database is isolated from other environments.
deployment_rules.workstation: Development workstation - configure production connection for testing plus local databases for isolation.
deployment_rules.testing_server: QA/Testing environment - includes failover options and backup connections.
deployment_rules.backup_server: Backup/Archive server - ONLY backup connections available.
deployment_rules.placeholder_skip: Connection entries with YOUR_* placeholders are automatically skipped by RemoteDB.pm

connection_priority_order: RemoteDB.pm tries connections in priority order (1-10). First available connection is used. Unreachable hosts are skipped after timeout.

production_server.notes: Production server connection - only configure in production environments

production_forager.notes: Optional forager database for distributed queries

production_server.notes: Production server accessible via local network

local_development.notes: Isolated development database for feature testing

local_testing.notes: Isolated test database for unit/integration tests

local_sqlite.notes: SQLite mode for testing without network access

zerotier_backup.notes: Backup server accessible via ZeroTier VPN

zerotier_archive.notes: Historical archive accessible via ZeroTier VPN

testing_local.description: Local testing database on QA server

zerotier_failover.notes: Failover connection for disaster recovery testing

backup_primary.notes: Main backup storage server - only configure in backup environments

production_deployment.guidance: On production servers, ONLY configure production connection profiles. Network isolation ensures no accidental connections to test systems.

development_workstation.guidance: Workstations can have production access plus local isolation for development. ZeroTier VPN connections available for remote testing.
development_workstation.recommended_priority: Use local_development as primary (priority 3) to avoid impacting production during normal development

qa_testing_server.guidance: QA servers should test against production data but with isolated test databases. Failover connections test disaster recovery.
qa_testing_server.recommended_priority: Production access (priority 1) for regression testing, local QA (priority 2) for new features

backup_archive_server.guidance: Backup servers ONLY have backup/archive connections. No access to production or development systems.

implementation_notes.environment_detection: RemoteDB.pm can auto-detect environment from hostname or ENV variables. Configure COMSERV_DEPLOYMENT_ENV=production|development|testing|backup
implementation_notes.connection_timeout: 5-second timeout applied to MariaDB/MySQL connections (after TASK 1 fix) to prevent indefinite hanging on unreachable hosts
implementation_notes.failover_strategy: If primary connection fails, RemoteDB.pm automatically tries next priority connection. No manual intervention needed.
implementation_notes.placeholder_handling: Entries with YOUR_* values are automatically skipped. This allows template to include all possible profiles with defaults.
implementation_notes.credentials_security: Store actual credentials in environment variables (/tmp/comserv_db_env.conf) rather than in this config file. Use Admin database_config form to manage.
